text: The surgeon performs a sub-total colectomy using a laparoscopic approach, with four trocars placed strategically, including sites for future ileostomy and colostomy. The transverse colon displays an inflammatory tubular appearance. The procedure begins with the release of the right colon, starting from the cecum and progressing towards the right colic flexure, moving from outside to inside.

The surgeon creates a small mesenteric breach in front of the ileocecal valve to facilitate the introduction of the endogea for the section of the ileal anus. After sectioning the ileal anus, the mesoappendix is sectioned, followed by the right mesocolon, while preserving the ileocolic vessels.

The surgeon continues the incision of the mesocolon from the bottom up to the right colic angle. Upon reaching the right colic angle, the operator sections the hepatocolic ligament using a left clavicle approach, facilitating the lowering and detachment of the thoracic angiocollis. The procedure progresses to the sectioning of the gastrocolic ligament from right to left, stopping at the level of the left colic angle.

The surgeon and assistant are positioned to the right of the patient, who is in a rolling position. The section of the left mesocolon is continued, facilitating the release of the left colon from the bottom up and from the outside in. The surgeon sections the colloparietal attachments, progressing towards the left colic angle for removal.

The surgeon carefully sections the lenocolic ligament, a delicate step due to the risk of bleeding. Following this, the left colic is completely detached. The section of the left mesocolon is continued from top to bottom, reaching the sigmoid mesocolon. The section is performed away from the sigmoid colon, targeting one or two proximal sigmoid arteries.

The surgeon sections the sigmoid mesocolon, moving towards the top of the sigmoid petal, ensuring a well-released and tensionless colostomy. The colon is freed from its attachments, and the extraction is performed through the hole in the left colon. The colostomy is placed, followed by the creation of the ileostomy, ensuring no twisting occurs.
